topics: 🪙 Bar Ziza's sharecropper: can a court argue claims the litigant himself never raised?
🔥 Mishnah on fire damage: spark, camel-with-flax, storekeeper's lamp
🕎 R' Yehudah: a Hanukkah light placed outside is פטור — מפני שהוא רשות
📜 New chapter: כפל is broader than ארבעה וחמשה — living and inanimate
🚫 גונב אחר הגנב, real estate, slaves, and documents are all excluded

overview.title: 📖 Overview — From Trustees to Thieves
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf opens with R' Yochanan's question — can a beit din advance an argument the party did not make? — illustrated by the strange Bar Ziza estate case. It then turns to the Mishnah of fire damage (spark, camel, storekeeper) and R' Yehudah's Hanukkah-light exemption. The page closes by opening a new chapter on theft: כפל covers everything alive or inanimate, while ארבעה וחמשה apply only to ox and sheep.
overview.hebrewSource: מְרוּבָּה מִידַּת תַּשְׁלוּמֵי כֶפֶל מִמִּידַּת תַּשְׁלוּמֵי אַרְבָּעָה וַחֲמִשָּׁה

slides.title: 🪙 The Bar Ziza Case
slides.subtitle: A sharecropper's deposit becomes a courtroom puzzle.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Bar Ziza's sharecropper deposits a pound of gold (לִיטְרָא דְהַב) with a third party.
2️⃣ Both Bar Ziza and the sharecropper die.
3️⃣ R' Yishmael b. R' Yose: everyone knows a sharecropper has nothing of his own — give it to Bar Ziza's sons; older sons take half now, younger half when they grow up.
4️⃣ R' Chiyya overrules: that argument לֵית שְׁמַע מִינָּהּ כְּלוּם — give it instead to the sharecropper's sons.
5️⃣ R' Yitzchak's framing: between older and younger sons it is כְּמִי שֶׁנִּיתַּן לָהֶן מַתָּנָה — treated as a gift, not a claim.

callout.label: Why this story is here:
callout.text: R' Yochanan asked whether a court can volunteer arguments the litigants themselves did not raise. The Bar Ziza incident is the test case — and it shows that what a beit din ruled stands, even after the next court rules differently.

slides.title: 🔥 Mishnah: Three Cases of Fire
slides.subtitle: Each scenario shifts liability based on whose action triggered the spread.

boxes.title: 🔨 הפטיש
items: Spark flies from a hammer and damages
Worker is liable — his act caused the spark

boxes.title: 🐪 הגמל
items: Camel loaded with flax in רשות הרבים
Flax enters a shop and ignites on the lamp
Camel owner is liable — he overloaded into the shop

boxes.title: 🪔 החנווני
items: If the storekeeper put his lamp outside

callout.label: Pivot point:
callout.text: The Mishnah moves liability case by case based on whose normal-use act exposed the property to fire. R' Yehudah's exemption for a Hanukkah light raises the harder question: can a mitzvah excuse you from damage liability?

slides.title: 💬 Hanukkah Light: Permission or Liability?
slides.subtitle: R' Yehudah and the Sages disagree on whether רשות frees you from damages.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: בְּנֵר חֲנוּכָּה פָטוּר מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהוּא רְשׁוּת — he placed it outside with permission of the mitzvah, so he is exempt.

lines.speaker: Ḥ
lines.text: The Sages: בֵּין כָּךְ וּבֵין כָּךְ חַיָיב — even when permitted, you remain liable for resulting damage.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Parallel: people build sukkot in front of their stores on חג — that too is permitted, yet if a passerby is hurt, the builder pays.

callout.label: The principle:
callout.text: Permission to perform an act (mitzvah or commercial) does not transfer the risk of damage onto the public. The actor still owns the consequences — except, per R' Yehudah, where the mitzvah itself authorizes the exposure.

slides.title: 📜 New Mishnah — Why כפל is Broader
slides.subtitle: The opening of chapter 7 contrasts the two penalty categories.

columns.label: ד׳ וה׳
columns.content:
Only שור ושה
כִּי יִגְנֹב אִישׁ שׁוֹר אוֹ שֶׂה

nodes.content: כפל is the default penalty for theft; ארבעה וחמשה is the narrow add-on for slaughter or sale of an ox or sheep.

callout.label: Source-driven move:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi derives the breadth of כפל from אִם הִמָּצֵא תִמָּצֵא... חַיִּים — 'alive' includes all living things, 'the stolen goods' includes all movables.

slides.title: 🚫 What is Excluded from כפל
slides.subtitle: R' Yishmael's three exclusions.
rows: The first thief already 'owns' the loss

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: The Torah's gnevah penalty fastens onto a movable, valuable, ownerless-once-stolen object. Anything that fails one of those tests drops out of the כפל system.

summary.principle: The daf bridges three sugyot: a court can sometimes act on arguments not raised; fire liability tracks whose action exposed the property; and theft's penalty system distinguishes broad (כפל) from narrow (ארבעה וחמשה).

columns.content:
🪙 Bar Ziza
court argues for the silent party

📜 כפל vs. ד׳ וה׳
broad vs. narrow penalty

flowSteps.content: Each move shows the Yerushalmi anchoring liability — and exemption — in source verses and prior court rulings.

🎓 Key Principle:
What a beit din ruled stands; permitted acts do not erase damage liability; and the Torah's penalty scope is exactly what the verses specify.

quiz.q: In the Bar Ziza incident, why did R' Yishmael b. R' Yose order the gold given to Bar Ziza's sons?
o: Because the sharecropper had no children
Because everyone knows a sharecropper's property belongs to his master
Because the trustee had already disbursed half
Because gold cannot be inherited
quiz.e: מָאן הוּא דְלָא יְדַע דְּכָל מַאי דְאִית לָאֲרִיסֵיהּ דְּבַר זִיזָא לְבַר זִיזָא אִינּוּן — a sharecropper holds his master's property.

quiz.q: When R' Chiyya later overruled R' Yishmael b. R' Yose, what did he say about the half already disbursed?
o: It must be returned
מַה שֶׁנָּתַתָּה עַל פִּי בֵּית דִּין נָתַתָּה — what you gave by court order stands
It is treated as a loan
It must be split between both families
quiz.e: A court ruling, even one later overturned, protects what was disbursed. The remaining half goes by the new ruling.

quiz.q: Whom does the Mishnah hold liable when a camel's flax catches fire on a storekeeper's lamp?
o: The storekeeper, always
Nobody — both acted with permission
The camel's owner, since his flax entered the shop
The municipality
quiz.e: בַּעַל הַגָּמָל חַיָיב — the camel owner overloaded into the shop. (Only if the storekeeper put his lamp outside does liability shift to him.)

quiz.q: What is R' Yehudah's reason for exempting damage from a Hanukkah light placed outside?
o: It is too small to cause real damage
מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהוּא רְשׁוּת — the mitzvah authorizes its placement
Hanukkah is biblical and overrides נזיקין
Insurance covers it
quiz.e: R' Yehudah holds that mitzvah-authorized placement removes liability. The Sages disagree and use the example of sukkah-stalls in front of stores to refute him.

quiz.q: Which of the following is NOT excluded by R' Yishmael from the law of כפל?
o: Real estate (קרקעות)
Slaves (עבדים)
Documents (שטרות)
A donkey (חמור)
quiz.e: Land, slaves, and documents are excluded; a donkey is movable and alive, so it is included in כפל. The Torah even mentions שׁוֹר וַחֲמוֹר וָשֶׂה.
